data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: Design principle that predicts the time required to move to a target area, emphasizing clickable items’ size and distance.
data.technical_spec: Optimization of interactive elements to be large enough and appropriately spaced.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: The time it takes to make a decision increases with the number and complexity of choices.
data.technical_spec: Simplified navigation structure and reduced options on interfaces.

data.title: Law of Common Region
data.description: Elements tend to be perceived as grouped if they are located within the same closed region.
data.technical_spec: Use of color, borders, or background to logically group related items.

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Users perceive objects that are close to each other as related more than objects that are further apart.
data.technical_spec: Strategic use of whitespace and proximity to relate UI components.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other sites; they prefer your site to work the same way.
data.technical_spec: Adherence to standard web practices that meet user expectations.
